task_completion.description: The agent opened the community meal train spreadsheet in a spreadsheet application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Calc or a similar spreadsheet application is open, displaying columns such as Date, Volunteer_Name, Dish_Planned, and Contains_Meat.

task_completion.description: The duplicate entries for March 3 and March 11 are resolved so that each date appears only once.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Date column, March 3, 2025 and March 11, 2025 each appear in exactly one row. The extra rows for these dates have been deleted or moved to other dates.

task_completion.description: New meal entries are added for the previously missing dates, March 7 and March 10.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet contains rows for March 7, 2025 and March 10, 2025, complete with a volunteer name, a planned dish, and 'No' in the Contains_Meat column.

task_completion.description: The dietary violations on March 5 and March 9 are corrected to be vegetarian.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rows for March 5 and March 9 no longer list 'Meatloaf' or 'BBQ Chicken' (or they are changed to vegetarian alternatives), and the Contains_Meat column for these dates is set to 'No'.

task_completion.description: The final spreadsheet contains exactly 14 consecutive days of vegetarian meals from March 1 to March 14.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet has exactly 14 data rows (plus the header) covering March 1 through March 14 consecutively without any missing dates. All 14 rows have 'No' in the Contains_Meat column.

integrity.description: The agent edited the spreadsheet using the graphical user interface of LibreOffice Calc.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreOffice Calc window active, with cells being selected, edited, or rows being deleted/inserted manually.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass manual spreadsheet editing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run Python scripts, sed, awk, or other command-line tools to directly modify the CSV file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial dataset covers March 1-14, 2025 but contains intentional errors: 1) Duplicates: March 3 and March 11 each have 2 signups. 2) Gaps: March 7 and March 10 are missing. 3) Dietary violations: March 5 (Meatloaf) and March 9 (BBQ Chicken) are marked Contains_Meat='Yes'. A correct solution will show a spreadsheet with exactly 14 data rows for March 1-14, one for each date, with all Contains_Meat values set to 'No'.
